pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 10, export_result.sh line 8 - consistent path configuration
setup_task.sh line 11, export_result.sh line 9 - consistent path configuration
export_result.sh line 64 and verifier.py line 64 both check 'if count >= 12'
export_result.sh line 77 and verifier.py line 77 both check 'if size > 100 * 1024' which equals 'size > 102400', meaning the actual minimum is 102401 bytes

Task description mentions '180 degrees of rotation' but export_result.sh and verifier.py contain no validation of rotation angle in degrees. Verifier only checks binary visibility of rotation via VLM (line 150 of verifier.py)

privileged_info_summary: The task loads the real CT Cranium DICOM dataset from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip (25.5 MB, 8732 downloads). The minimum frame count is verified as 12 frames. The minimum video file size is verified as 102401 bytes (not 102400 as metadata claims). The required output paths are /home/ga/Documents/skull_rotation_frames for frames or /home/ga/Documents/skull_rotation.avi for video. No code validation exists for the claimed 180-degree rotation angle minimum.
